label: Eq. 1
verbatim: y(x) = sum_{i=1}^{N} pi(x)_i E_i(x), where pi(x) = Mask(sigma(x), top-k) in R^N are the masked gate logits set to zero for logits not in the top-k of sigma(x)=Softmax(g(x)); top-k << N, e.g. N=128 and top-k=8 in Qwen3.
paraphrase: Standard MoE forward: output is sum over experts of masked softmax gate weight times expert output, with top-k routing.
preconditions: Per-token router scores g(x)=xW_g; softmax then top-k mask.
consequences: Defines the layer output that REAM tries to preserve under merging.

label: Eq. 2
verbatim: S^freq_i = (1/|X|) sum_{x in X} 1[i in Top-k(sigma(x))]
paraphrase: Frequency saliency: fraction of calibration tokens for which expert i is among top-k.
consequences: Baseline saliency criterion (used by Freq); criticized as ignoring contribution magnitude.

label: Eq. 3 (REAP saliency)
verbatim: S^reap_i = (1/|X_i|) sum_{x in X_i} pi(x)_i * ||E_i(x)||_2 , where X_i in X is the set of tokens where expert i is active.
paraphrase: REAP saliency: average over tokens that activate expert i of router weight times L2 norm of the expert output.
preconditions: Top-k masked router weight pi(x)_i; expert output norm computed on calibration set.
consequences: REAM uses this S^reap_i to select centroid experts; key dependency for Stage 2 merging.

label: Eq. 4
verbatim: delta_E(i,j) = (1/|X|) sum_{x in X} sim(E_i(x), E_j(x))
paraphrase: Output-based expert similarity averaged over calibration set, e.g., via cosine similarity.
consequences: Baseline similarity used pre-REAM; REAM extends this with gate scaling (Eq. 8).

label: Eq. 5 (gate-logit similarity)
paraphrase: Similarity between two experts based on the vector of router logits across the calibration set.
preconditions: Per-token gate logits g(x)_i in R.
consequences: Used as one of the two summands in REAM's combined similarity (Eq. 7).

label: Eq. 6 (frequency-weighted merge)
verbatim: W_merged = (sum_{i=1}^{C} S^freq_i W_i) / (sum_{i=1}^{C} S^freq_i), where W_i are expert i's weight matrices with neuron permutation alignment (Ainsworth et al., 2023) applied w.r.t. the dominant (centroid) expert.
paraphrase: Per-group merged weight is frequency-saliency weighted average of permutation-aligned expert weights with respect to the centroid.
preconditions: Group of C experts, neuron alignment to centroid.
consequences: Reference merge formula; REAM's actual saliency-weighted merge uses S^reap (per Eq. 3) by extension.

label: Eq. 7 (REAM aggregated similarity)
verbatim: delta_REAM(i,j) = delta_g(i,j) + tilde-delta_E(i,j)
paraphrase: REAM's expert similarity is the sum of gate-logit similarity (Eq. 5) and gated expert-output similarity (Eq. 8).
preconditions: Both component similarities computed on the same calibration set.
consequences: Used by greedy pseudo-pruning to assign non-centroid experts to nearest centroid.

label: Eq. 8 (gated expert similarity)
verbatim: tilde-delta_E(i,j) = (1/|X|) sum_{x in X} sim(sigma(x)_i E_i(x), sigma(x)_j E_j(x))
paraphrase: Gated output similarity uses softmax-scaled expert outputs sigma(x)_i*E_i(x), matching the actual MoE output term in Eq. 1.
preconditions: Softmax probabilities sigma(x) (NOT top-k masked) and expert outputs.
consequences: Removing the sigma scaling (i.e., reverting to Eq. 4) is the second-largest ablation drop (Delta_AVG = -5.9, Delta_GEN = -11.5).

label: Pseudo-pruning grouping
verbatim: Given REAP saliency scores S^reap_i computed over a calibration set X, we group the N experts into N' < N clusters via a greedy pseudo-pruning procedure. We follow Li et al. (2024) and for each layer ell, we designate the N' experts with the highest saliency as the cluster centroids C_ell = {c_1,...,c_N'}, but we sort them in decreasing order of saliency. Then, starting from c_1, we greedily assign to it up to C unassigned non-centroid experts E_j that are most similar to c_1 based on delta_REAM(c_1,j) in Eq. (7).
paraphrase: Greedy procedure: pick top-N' saliency experts as centroids (decreasing order); iterate centroids c_1,c_2,...; each absorbs up to C nearest non-centroids by delta_REAM; remaining non-centroids continue to next centroid.
preconditions: REAP saliency S^reap, REAM similarity delta_REAM, group-size cap C.
consequences: Defines max_merge_group = C; cited by spec D5 row(s).

label: Pseudo-pruning leaves many singletons
verbatim: Since typically N - N' << N' . C (e.g., N' is 25% smaller than N), the set of non-centroid experts is far smaller than the total absorption capacity of all centroids, so most centroids receive no assignments and form singleton groups that pass through unchanged. Accordingly, we call our grouping method pseudo-pruning.
paraphrase: Because total absorption capacity N'*C far exceeds N-N', most centroids stay singletons; only a few large groups form. Thus REAM behaves like pruning for most experts.
preconditions: C set 16/32; 25% or 50% reduction; N' centroids.
consequences: Justifies name 'pseudo-pruning'; explains why removing pseudo-prune costs Delta_AVG=-3.6.

label: Group size C — Qwen3-30B 96 experts
verbatim: The only hyperparameter of REAM is group size C of pseudo-pruning ... fixed to 16 for Qwen3-30B-A3B-Instruct-2507 (when compressed to 96 experts)
paraphrase: C=16 for Qwen3-30B-A3B-Instruct-2507 at 25% reduction (128->96 experts).
consequences: Spec must use C=16 in this configuration to be paper-faithful.

label: Group size C — Qwen3-30B 64 experts
verbatim: fixed to ... 32 (when compressed to 64 experts)
paraphrase: C=32 for Qwen3-30B-A3B-Instruct-2507 at 50% reduction (128->64 experts).
consequences: Sets max_merge_group=32 at 50% compression for Qwen3-30B.

label: Group size C — Qwen3-Coder-Next / Qwen3-Next-80B
verbatim: 32 for Qwen3-Coder-Next and Qwen3-Next-80B-A3B-Instruct
paraphrase: C=32 for both 80B Qwen3 variants (512->384 experts at 25% reduction).
consequences: Larger N => larger C.

label: Group size C — GLM-4.5-Air
verbatim: and to 16 for GLM-4.5-Air
paraphrase: C=16 for GLM-4.5-Air (128->96 experts at 25% reduction).
consequences: Same C as Qwen3-30B at the same N and compression ratio.

label: Calibration set size
verbatim: we collect router logits and expert activations on a mixture of three datasets with 3072 sequences of 512 tokens each — C4 (Raffel et al., 2019) for general language understanding, NuminaMath (LI et al., 2024) for mathematical reasoning, and The-Stack-Smol (Kocetkov et al., 2022) for code generation.
paraphrase: Calibration: 3072 sequences x 512 tokens (~1.57M tokens) drawn from a mixture of C4, NuminaMath, The-Stack-Smol.
preconditions: Pre-tokenization to 512-length; mixing ratios per Table 3.
consequences: Stage 2 calibration size budget.

label: Table 3 — calibration mixing ratios
paraphrase: Ten C4:Math:Code mixing ratios used in main analysis.
consequences: Pareto/HV/correlation experiments use exactly these 10 ratios.

label: Default mix for 25% reduction (Table 2)
verbatim: we fix the mixture at a code heavy ratio of 0 : 0.3 : 0.7 to favor the overall GEN score
paraphrase: For larger-model experiments (Table 2) the calibration mixture is fixed at C4:Math:Code = 0:0.3:0.7.
consequences: Choice for spec when GEN-favoring is required.

label: Combined cost matrix C<ci,j>
verbatim: C<ci,j> = C_act + C_wt in R^{d x d}. Here, [C_act]_{pq} = ||bar-H^p_{c_i} - bar-H^q_j||_2 is the distance between the normalized calibration-token activation vectors of the p-th and q-th neurons across the two experts, and [C_wt]_{pq} = ||W^p_{c_i} - W^q_j||_2 is the distance between their weight matrices W^{(p)}_i.
paraphrase: REAM's intermediate-neuron alignment cost is sum of an activation-vector L2 distance term and a weight-vector L2 distance term, both indexed over neurons p,q.
preconditions: Normalized activations bar-H per neuron; weight columns/rows W^p; per-pair (centroid, expert).
consequences: Solved by Hungarian algorithm to give optimal neuron permutation; cited by D5a/D5b spec rows.

label: Hungarian neuron alignment
verbatim: Li et al. (2024) used the Hungarian algorithm with the cost matrix C_wt computed based on the distances between the weights of the centroid expert c_i and expert j. To improve the alignment, we introduce a combined cost matrix C<ci,j> = C_act + C_wt ... The optimal permutation is then applied to reorder the weights of expert j.
paraphrase: Hungarian algorithm finds optimal d-dim neuron permutation minimizing combined activation+weight cost; result reorders expert j's weights to align with centroid c_i prior to weighted-average merge.
preconditions: C<ci,j> in R^{d x d}; one Hungarian solve per non-centroid expert per group.
consequences: Pre-merge alignment step; spec must run Hungarian, not e.g. random/identity permutation.

label: Activation-only alignment ablation
verbatim: replacing the combined activation and weight alignment C<ci,j> with activation-only alignment C_act yields the smallest penalty (Delta_AVG = -0.5)
paraphrase: Dropping C_wt and using activation-only cost loses ~0.5 AVG; weight cost contributes a small but consistent regularization.
consequences: Weight cost matrix is required for paper-faithful behavior, even though impact is marginal.

label: Sequential merging
verbatim: after merging layer ell, a second forward pass is run through this layer to recompute its activations to be used by the subsequent layer ell+1. This ensures that each layer's statistics reflect the actual input it will receive at inference time.
paraphrase: After merging an MoE layer, re-run a forward pass through that layer to recompute activations feeding into the next layer; subsequent layer's statistics use the merged-layer outputs, not the original-model outputs.
preconditions: Layer-by-layer merge; two forward passes per merged layer (one to collect, one to recompute outputs).
consequences: Spec Stage 2 must do per-layer activation recomputation; non-sequential merging is the most damaging GEN ablation (-3.9 GEN avg).

label: Sequential merging runtime overhead
verbatim: For Qwen3-30B-A3B-Instruct-2507, non-sequential merging takes ~1 hour, while our sequential variant takes ~1.5 hours, with ~30 GB of VRAM in both cases.
paraphrase: Sequential vs. non-sequential merging: ~1.5h vs ~1h on Qwen3-30B; ~30 GB VRAM either way.
consequences: Spec H200 timing/VRAM forecasts depend on this paper-side anchor.

label: REAM minus all components = MC-SMoE
verbatim: Removing all our components together would make REAM equivalent to MC-SMoE (Li et al., 2024).
paraphrase: REAM = MC-SMoE + 4 additions: REAP saliency, gated expert similarity, pseudo-pruning grouping, sequential merging, combined cost matrix.
consequences: Identifies upstream baseline.

label: Gate row removal (REAP-style)
verbatim: In our work, we follow REAP and remove the rows of the gate weights that are not corresponding to centroid experts.
paraphrase: After merging, the gate weight matrix W_g has its non-centroid rows removed (kept only rows for the N' centroid experts), as in REAP. Merging methods' alternative — keeping all gate rows and summing logits per group — has irreducible error.
preconditions: Centroid set C_ell of size N'.
consequences: Spec must drop non-centroid gate rows after merge; this is REAP-style not classic-merge.

label: Top-k preserved post-compression
verbatim: Qwen3-30B-A3B-Instruct-2507, a 30B-parameter MoE model with N=128 experts per layer, of which top-k=8 are active per token.
paraphrase: Qwen3-30B uses N=128, top-k=8; merging reduces N but does not change top-k.
consequences: Spec must preserve top-k routing config.

label: No post-compression fine-tuning
verbatim: We follow evaluation in REAP (Lasby et al., 2025) and evaluate all methods without any fine-tuning after compression.
paraphrase: REAM is evaluated with no post-compression fine-tuning, matching REAP's protocol.
consequences: Spec Stage 2 produces a usable model without recovery training (Stage 3 KD is separate).

label: Compression regimes 25% and 50%
verbatim: We compress models by merging 25% or 50% of the experts per layer, e.g., reducing from 128 to 96 or 64 experts, respectively.
paraphrase: Two regimes: 25% reduction (N=128 -> N'=96) and 50% reduction (N=128 -> N'=64).
consequences: Default REAM compression ratios.

label: Table 1 — REAM at 96 experts
verbatim: REAM 96 0:0.5:0.5 IFEval 89.9 AIME25 60.0 GSM8K 86.3 GPQA 38.4 HumanEval 93.3 LCB 51.0 GEN 69.8
paraphrase: REAM at 25% reduction (96 experts) on Qwen3-30B with best mix 0:0.5:0.5 attains GEN=69.8 vs. uncompressed 70.9.
consequences: Anchor for empirical replication validation.

label: Table 1 — REAP at 96 experts
verbatim: REAP 96 0.2:0.25:0.55 IFEval 89.6 AIME25 50.0 GSM8K 87.9 GPQA 39.4 HumanEval 94.5 LCB 50.3 GEN 68.6
paraphrase: REAP best at 96 experts: GEN=68.6 with mix 0.2:0.25:0.55.
consequences: Comparator for REAM.

label: Table 2 — REAM Qwen3-80B 384 experts
verbatim: Qwen3-80B-A3B REAM 384 IFEval 93.4 AIME25 73.3 GSM8K 78.1 GPQA 46.5 HumanEval 93.9 LCB 43.7 GEN 71.5; Original 512 GEN 72.9
paraphrase: REAM at 25% reduction on Qwen3-Next-80B-A3B-Instruct: GEN 71.5 vs. uncompressed 72.9.
consequences: Anchor for 80B replication.

label: Table 2 — REAM GLM-4.5-Air
verbatim: GLM-4.5-Air REAM 96 IFEval 83.6 AIME25 83.3 GSM8K 94.9 GPQA 37.9 HumanEval 90.2 LCB 53.7 GEN 73.9; Original 128 GEN 77.1
paraphrase: REAM at 25% on GLM-4.5-Air: GEN 73.9 vs. 77.1 uncompressed.
consequences: GLM-family anchor.

label: Ablation — REAP saliency replaced by freq
verbatim: We observe the largest single degradation (Delta_AVG = -8.7) to come from replacing REAP's saliency score (Eq. (3)) with routing frequency (Eq. (2)).
paraphrase: Replacing S^reap by S^freq is the single most damaging ablation: -8.7 AVG.
consequences: REAM's centroid-selection saliency MUST be REAP saliency, not frequency.

label: Ablation — w/o gated experts sim (sigma scaling)
verbatim: removing gate softmax scaling (sigma(x) in Eq. (8)) before computing pairwise output similarity (Delta_AVG = -5.9, Delta_GEN = -11.5)
paraphrase: If output similarity uses raw E_i(x) instead of sigma(x)*E_i(x), GEN drops 11.5 points.
consequences: Sigma-scaling in Eq. 8 is essential.

label: Ablation — w/o pseudo-prune
verbatim: removing pseudopruning to incur a moderate penalty (Delta_AVG = -3.6)
paraphrase: Replacing pseudo-pruning with MC-SMoE-style grouping costs 3.6 AVG.
consequences: Pseudo-pruning grouping is required.

label: Ablation — w/o gate-logit similarity delta_g
verbatim: the expert co-activation signals from gate logit similarity (delta_g in Eq. (7)) ... contributing smaller but consistent gains of Delta_AVG = -1.4
paraphrase: Dropping delta_g costs 1.4 AVG.
consequences: delta_g contribution is small but real.

label: Ablation — w/o sequential merge
verbatim: the re-computation of activations from sequential merging to be each contributing smaller but consistent gains of ... -1.0
paraphrase: Sequential merging is worth ~1.0 AVG (more on GEN: -3.9 in coder ablation table).
consequences: Sequential merging mandatory for paper-faithful spec.

label: Ablation — only align activations (drop C_wt)
verbatim: replacing the combined activation and weight alignment C<ci,j> with activation-only alignment C_act yields the smallest penalty (Delta_AVG = -0.5)
paraphrase: Dropping the weight component C_wt loses 0.5 AVG.
consequences: Weight cost still required for full paper-faithful behavior.

label: Pareto / HV at 96 experts
paraphrase: At 96 experts, REAM has highest hypervolume (920.3) and tied-highest Pareto-optimal-ratios fraction (7/10).
consequences: Method-level summary stat.

label: Pareto / HV at 64 experts
paraphrase: At 64 experts, REAP slightly higher HV (931.4) than REAM (910.7); REAM still has most Pareto-optimal ratios (7/10).
consequences: REAP closer at 50% compression.

label: C4 mass correlated with MC, anti-with GEN
verbatim: for Freq, REAP, and REAM, the proportion of C4 data in the calibration mixture is strongly positively correlated with MC scores (r >= 0.95) yet strongly negatively correlated with GEN scores (r <= -0.82)
paraphrase: Higher C4 share predicts MC up, GEN down for saliency-based methods.
consequences: Calibration is the lever for MC<->GEN trade-off.

label: Pre-logit rank correlation
verbatim: REAM has the steepest and tightest regression curve where rank is an excellent predictor of performance ... REAM (r=0.95, p=0.000)
paraphrase: Average pre-logit numerical rank strongly correlates (r=0.95) with REAM's downstream performance.
consequences: Suggests rank as cheap proxy to choose mixture.

label: Four REAM components
verbatim: REAM, a unified expert compression framework with four key components ... (1) an expert similarity metric that combines gate logit similarities with softmax-scaled expert output similarities ... (2) a pseudo-pruning strategy that produces a few large groups and many singletons simultaneously; (3) enhanced weight alignment through a more informed cost matrix using both activation-based and weight-based costs; (4) a sequential merging procedure that recomputes forward-pass statistics after each layer is merged.
paraphrase: REAM = (1) delta_REAM similarity, (2) pseudo-pruning grouping, (3) C_act+C_wt cost matrix, (4) sequential merging.
preconditions: All four needed for full REAM.
consequences: Spec Stage 2 must implement all four to claim REAM-faithful.

label: Centroid sorting in decreasing saliency
verbatim: we designate the N' experts with the highest saliency as the cluster centroids C_ell = {c_1,...,c_N'}, but we sort them in decreasing order of saliency. Then, starting from c_1, we greedily assign to it up to C unassigned non-centroid experts E_j that are most similar to c_1
paraphrase: Centroids must be processed in decreasing saliency order; greedy assignment starts from the highest-saliency centroid.
consequences: Order matters; spec must replicate.

label: max_merge_group cap C semantics
verbatim: we greedily assign to it up to C unassigned non-centroid experts E_j
paraphrase: Cap C is the maximum group size — i.e., max number of non-centroid experts a single centroid may absorb.
consequences: Defines max_merge_group used by spec.

label: Per-layer centroid set
verbatim: for each layer ell, we designate the N' experts with the highest saliency as the cluster centroids C_ell
paraphrase: Centroid selection and grouping are independent per layer ell.
consequences: Spec processes layers independently for grouping; sequential merge introduces cross-layer dependence in *statistics*, not in centroid selection.

label: Evaluation harness — lm-eval-harness + vLLM
verbatim: For evaluation we use EleutherAI Language Model Evaluation Harness (Gao et al., 2021) with a HuggingFace or vLLM backend (Kwon et al., 2023) and default task settings.
paraphrase: Eval is lm-eval-harness with HF or vLLM backend, default settings.
consequences: Audit eval bench should match this stack.

label: GPQA-Diamond — no CoT, 5-shot
verbatim: GPQA-Diamond is evaluated without chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning using 5 shots.
paraphrase: GPQA-Diamond: 5-shot, no CoT.
consequences: Spec must not use CoT for GPQA-Diamond.

label: LiveCodeBench v6 official
verbatim: For LiveCodeBench-v6 we use their official evaluation code.
paraphrase: LiveCodeBench-v6 is evaluated via the official LCB evaluation code, not via lm-eval-harness.
consequences: Spec eval pipeline must integrate LCB v6 official.

label: GLM-4.5-Air uses glm-simple-evals for HumanEval/LCB
verbatim: to evaluation GLM-4.5-Air on HumanEval and LiveCodeBench we use the evaluation tool from https://github.com/zai-org/glm-simple-evals.
paraphrase: For GLM-4.5-Air on HumanEval and LiveCodeBench, evaluation uses glm-simple-evals.
consequences: Spec must distinguish GLM eval pipeline.

label: MC suite (8 tasks)
verbatim: WinoGrande, the Challenge and Easy set in AI2 Reasoning Challenge (ARC), BoolQ, HellaSwag, MMLU, OpenBookQA, and Recognizing Textual Entailment (RTE).
paraphrase: Eight MC tasks: WinoGrande, ARC-Challenge, ARC-Easy, BoolQ, HellaSwag, MMLU, OpenBookQA, RTE.
consequences: Spec MC bench list.

label: GEN suite (6 tasks)
verbatim: IFEval, AIME25, GSM8K, HumanEval, GPQA-Diamond, and LiveCodeBench-v6.
paraphrase: Six GEN tasks.
preconditions: Default settings except GPQA/LCB exceptions above.
consequences: Spec GEN bench list.

label: Calibration acts as implicit prior
verbatim: expert merging is fundamentally a data-driven procedure ... both the saliency scores S^reap_i and the pairwise similarities delta(c_i,j) are computed entirely from activations on the calibration set X, thus making the merging decisions an implicit function of the calibration distribution.
paraphrase: Both saliency and similarity are functions only of calibration activations.
consequences: Calibration set must be representative of target task distribution.

label: Models evaluated
verbatim: Qwen3-30B-A3B-Instruct-2507 ... Qwen3-Coder-Next and Qwen3-Next-80B-A3B-Instruct ... GLM-4.5-Air, a 106B-parameter model with 128 experts per layer.
paraphrase: REAM evaluated on Qwen3-30B-A3B-Instruct-2507 (128 experts), Qwen3-Coder-Next & Qwen3-Next-80B-A3B-Instruct (512 experts), GLM-4.5-Air (128 experts, 106B).
consequences: Spec compatibility list.

label: Static, no dynamic retraining
verbatim: Our work is along the static direction, which is more pragmatic than a dynamic one ... no additional training
paraphrase: REAM is purely a one-shot static transformation; no router-finetuning or training-time updates.
consequences: Spec Stage 2 is training-free.

label: Activation vectors normalized for C_act
verbatim: [C_act]_{pq} = ||bar-H^p_{c_i} - bar-H^q_j||_2 is the distance between the normalized calibration-token activation vectors
paraphrase: Per-neuron activation vectors bar-H are normalized before computing the C_act distance.
consequences: Spec must normalize activation vectors before C_act.

label: Singleton centroids unchanged
verbatim: most centroids receive no assignments and form singleton groups that pass through unchanged.
paraphrase: Singleton centroids (group size 1) are not modified by REAM — they pass through with their original weights.
consequences: Numerical equivalence: singleton experts must remain bit-identical.

label: Merge weights use saliency (REAP) weights
verbatim: we follow Li et al. (2024) and ... saliency-weighted merging
paraphrase: REAM merges per-group with saliency-weighted average; the saliency used is REAP saliency (Eq. 3), since centroid selection is REAP-saliency-based and §1 lists it as a component.
consequences: Spec merge formula combines Eq. 6 form with S^reap (not S^freq).

label: 3072 sequences x 512 tokens
verbatim: 3072 sequences of 512 tokens each
paraphrase: Calibration corpus: 3072 packed sequences each exactly 512 tokens.
preconditions: Mixed across 3 sources per Table 3 ratio.
consequences: Sets the |X| in all expectations.

label: Ablation reference setting
verbatim: Fig. 4a reports the effect of removing each REAM component in isolation at 96 experts with a GEN-favoring calibration mixture of 0.1:0.1:0.8.
paraphrase: All single-component ablations of REAM are run at 96 experts on Qwen3-30B-A3B-Instruct-2507 with mix 0.1:0.1:0.8.
consequences: Reference point for cross-validation of component importances.
